name: Olwen
description: Olwen, meaning "white footprint," is a maiden from Welsh mythology, primarily known from the tale of Culhwch and Olwen in the Mabinogion. She is the daughter of the giant Ysbaddaden Chief-Giant, and her beauty is legendary. Wherever she walks, white trefoils spring up, symbolizing her purity and grace. Culhwch, upon hearing of her unparalleled beauty from his stepmother, is immediately smitten and embarks on a perilous quest to win her hand in marriage. This quest involves completing a series of incredibly difficult and seemingly impossible tasks set by her father, who is destined to die if Olwen ever marries. These tasks require the assistance of King Arthur and his knights, making Olwen central to a narrative celebrating Arthurian heroism and Welsh folklore. Her significance lies not only in her beauty, but also in her role as the catalyst for a grand adventure that showcases the courage, loyalty, and magical prowess of the Arthurian heroes. She represents the ultimate prize, the embodiment of feminine beauty and grace, worth risking life and limb to attain. The story of Culhwch and Olwen is a rich tapestry of Welsh mythology, and Olwen remains a pivotal figure, embodying the ideals of beauty and virtue. Her name echoes through the tales of Welsh folklore, forever linked to the trials and triumphs of Culhwch and the Knights of the Round Table as they navigate a world of giants, magic, and impossible tasks.
subtitle: The Maiden with the White Footprint

keyMyths.title: The Quest for Olwen
keyMyths.description: Culhwch, driven by love, seeks Arthur's help to win Olwen's hand. He must complete impossible tasks set by Olwen's father, Ysbaddaden Chief-Giant, to succeed.

keyMyths.title: Olwen's Beauty
keyMyths.description: Olwen's beauty is so radiant that white trefoils spring up wherever she walks. This signifies her purity and makes her the object of Culhwch's desire.

keyMyths.title: Ysbaddaden's Fate
keyMyths.description: Ysbaddaden is destined to die if Olwen marries. He sets impossible tasks hoping to prevent the marriage and prolong his life.

sources.author: Gwyn Jones and Thomas Jones
sources.description: A collection of early Welsh prose stories, including Culhwch and Olwen.
sources.text: A collection of early Welsh prose stories, including Culhwch and Olwen.
